judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully and correctly answer all four questions with grounded git evidence; they are comparable, with Report 1 and Report 2 each hitting every expectation.

report_1.evidence: **`main`.** The branch's only dependency — the `overlap()` token-overlap helper used by the new pairwise metric — was delivered by `feat/weighted-scorer`, and that work has already landed on `main`

report_2.evidence: **`main` (the trunk).** ... The branch's *actual* dependency base, `feat/weighted-scorer`, is a **dead base**: its content is already on `main`

report_1.evidence: It was branched from **`feat/weighted-scorer` at its tip `ac9d505` (W3)** — not from `main`. ... **`feat/weighted-scorer` has already been merged into `main`, via squash-merge**, as commit `1971f08 (#42)`. ... `ac9d505` (and `7b2e100`, `1bfecb6`) all **NOT in main**.

report_2.evidence: **It was created from `feat/weighted-scorer`, at that branch's tip `ac9d505`** ... its work has already landed on `main` under a different commit. - Not an ancestor: `git merge-base --is-ancestor feat/weighted-scorer origin/main` returns `1` ... The branch ref still exists on both local and `origin`. ... reached `main` as a squashed/refactored single commit (PR #42) with a *different SHA*

report_1.evidence: **No — not all of the three-dot diff is pairwise work.** ... `scoring/scorer.py` (+10/−3) ... That extra 16/3 is present **because the branch was cut from `feat/weighted-scorer` before that branch was squash-merged**. The three-dot diff is computed from the old merge-base `65204ad`, so it re-includes W1–W3

report_2.evidence: `scoring/scorer.py` — `+10/-3` ... → **entirely weighted-scorer work** (W1). **No — not all of it is pairwise work.** ... **Why that content is present:** ... the branch's merge-base with `main` is the *old root* `65204ad` ... A three-dot PR diff measured from that root therefore re-shows the weighted changes as if new

report_1.evidence: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric` ... After rebase, `git log --oneline main..feat/pairwise-metric` should list only the two replayed pairwise commits, and `git diff --stat main...feat/pairwise-metric` should now match the two-dot result (`grader.txt` +1, `metrics.py` +4, 5 insertions).

report_2.evidence: `git rebase --onto origin/main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric` ... `git diff origin/main...HEAD` → now only `grader.txt +1` and `metrics.py +4`; `scorer.py` gone. - Scope check ... `git log origin/main..HEAD -- scoring/scorer.py` → empty
